title: David Hockney Says ‘There’s Too Much Abstraction in the Art World’
description: The beloved British artist just opened a show at London's Serpentine Galleries.

title: Greek TV Auctioneer and Gallery Owner Arrested Following Investigation into Illegal Antiquities Trade
description: Giorgos Tsagarakis was arrested by the country’s Organized Crime Division last Friday.

title: UK Considers Charging Museum Entry, Canada’s Azrieli Foundation Stops Funding for Toronto Arts Foundation, and More: Morning Links for March 26, 2026
description: The UK government is exploring the option of charging foreign tourists for entry to national museums.

title: At Art Basel Hong Kong, Blue-Chips Report Flurry of 7-Figure Deals, While Others Lament ‘Slower Than Usual’ Sales
description: Cash flowed, but confidence trickled on Day 1 of Art Basel Hong Kong.

title: Pat Steir, Famed for Her Abstract ‘Waterfall’ Paintings, Dies at 87
description: Her signature style involved pouring paint from a ladder.

title: Yto Barrada Says France Had ‘Full Awareness’ of Her Views on Israel When It Chose Her for Venice Biennale
description: A prominent Jewish group denounced her for signing a letter against the Israeli Pavilion.

title: K-Pop Band BTS Performed Two Songs from New Album ‘ARIRANG’ at the Guggenheim on Wednesday Morning
description: The top-secret performance was related to BTS's appearance on "The Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon" on Wednesday night.

title: Elsa Schiaparelli Gets Her UK Museum Debut at the V&A, in a Show Featuring Dalí, Man Ray, and Picasso
description: Dalí’s ‘Lobster Telephone’ is on display near the Lobster dress that he helped design.

title: Hauser & Wirth Partner Cristopher Canizares Departs to Start Artist Agency
description: The model, Canizares said, is “less like CAA and more like a family office,” with an emphasis on discretion and long-term planning over visibility or scale.

title: Female Pharaoh’s Erasure From Memory Was Not Revenge, Researcher Says
description: One researcher suggested that damage to sculptures of Hatshepsut had other causes.
